lead.title: Zelensky Dismisses Ukraine’s Top General After Protests
lead.snippet: The move followed the firing of a popular defense minister, who had clashed with the general over how to fight the war.

items.title: Inside the Fight to Rid South Africa of Black Immigrants
items.snippet: As immigration surges in the country, activists are going to extreme lengths to expel foreign nationals, often taking the law into their own hands.

items.title: 3 Myths Fueling Anti-Immigrant Sentiment in South Africa
items.snippet: As the nation convulses with protests and violence, data and research suggest that some of the biggest arguments against foreigners don’t stand up against the facts.

items.title: India’s Young Protesters Are Bloodied but Unbowed
items.snippet: A day after police forcibly broke up demonstrations, thousands returned to the protest site in New Delhi to keep pushing for action over the country’s failings in education.

items.title: Netanyahu Ad Uses a Questionable Tactic Against Bereaved Rival
items.snippet: A.I.-generated images showing Gadi Eisenkot, the Israeli leader’s top challenger in upcoming elections, played on anti-Arab sentiments and used images seen as disrespectful to his son, who was killed in the recent Gaza war.

items.title: British Politician Ann Widdecombe Was Murdered with a Hammer, Prosecutors Say
items.snippet: The politician, Ann Widdecombe, was eating lunch at home when a man walked in and asked whether she had bank cards, security footage showed.

items.title: Trump Escalates Canada Tariffs as Mark Carney Holds Firm
items.snippet: The president’s new tariffs on a top U.S. trading partner aim at gaining concessions, but the Canadian government, and the public, have been bracing for the escalation.

items.title: Houthis in Yemen Edge Closer to Entering U.S.-Iran War
items.snippet: The Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen has declared a Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, potentially opening a new front in the war rippling across the Middle East.

items.title: France Joins a Global Move Toward Restricting Social Media for Children
items.snippet: Its new law, the first of its kind enacted by a European country, will ban access to some accounts for anyone under 15.

items.title: The Teenage Boys In Search of Their Mother After the Venezuela Earthquake
items.snippet: After Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes, Winder and Deivi Delfín dug every day for 26 days to find the bodies of their mother, brother and grandmother.

items.title: When Protesters Speak, Zelensky Listens, in the Ukrainian Tradition
items.snippet: With anger high over the firing of the defense minister, the president’s office is signaling an openness to compromise.

items.title: A Family’s Daring Escape From North Korea
items.snippet: Their resolve for freedom hardened during the pandemic, when they lived through a crippling food shortage and were forced to witness frequent public executions.

items.title: New UK Prime Minister Burnham Promises Hope and Change. The Hurdles Are High.
items.snippet: Britain’s new prime minister, Andy Burnham, promised policies that would give average Britons financial “breathing space.” His opponents are already doling out criticism.

items.title: Philippines Says Sailor Was Injured in Skirmish With Chinese Coast Guard
items.snippet: China said it was not the aggressor in the episode, which was the latest flare-up in the South China Sea between the two countries.

items.title: Water Plants Under Fire in the Gulf Are Crucial to Civilians
items.snippet: Several days of airstrikes have hit water and energy plants in Kuwait, a nation that relies on desalination for 90 percent of its drinking water.

lead.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
lead.snippet: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its own pre-release models
items.snippet: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

items.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
items.snippet: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

items.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
items.snippet: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube,

items.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
items.snippet: Sila's fresh $300 million round will help it make enough of its silicon-carbon anode material to power more than 100,000 EVs.

items.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
items.snippet: The acquisition — Einride's first as a publicly traded company — will expand its EV-charging ecosystem as it works to scale its electric trucking business.

items.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
items.snippet: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

items.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
items.snippet: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

items.title: Tesla spins up robotaxi pilots in Orlando and Tampa ahead of Q2 earnings
items.snippet: The company didn't say how many are in each city and has taken a far more cautious approach to scaling the network than CEO Elon Musk had promised.

items.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
items.snippet: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

items.title: Instagram now lets you swap out the music in your old posts
items.snippet: With the new "Replace Audio" tool, users can update the music on their post at any time while keeping the post's existing likes, comments, shares, and reach intact.

items.title: Threads rolls out parental supervision tools
items.snippet: With the new tools, parents and guardians will be able to view their teen's time spent on Threads, set daily time limits, adjust sleep mode, and manage their privacy settings.

items.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
items.snippet: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

items.title: Light made a flip phone — it’s colorful and it’s cheap
items.snippet: Light co-founder Kaiwei Tang helped create the Motorola Razr, and he's as surprised as anyone that over 20 years later, the flip phone is relevant again.

items.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
items.snippet: TikTok recently settled its portion of the case with the plaintiff ahead of a jury trial that starts next week in Los Angeles. YouTube has also reached a deal, leaving Meta as the only remaining defendant.

lead.title: ΑΕΚ: Ο Κάνγκουα σκόραρε στην ήττα της Μπερ Σεβά στην Ισλανδία
lead.snippet: Ο Κινγκς Κάνγκουα που απασχολεί την ΑΕΚ χρίστηκε σκόρερ στην ήττα της Χάποελ Μπερ Σεβά από τη Βίκινγκουρ με 2-1.

items.title: Βίντεο: 22χρονος ορκίστηκε με μια φιάλη στον ώμο - Ήθελε να τιμήσει τα 26 χρόνια σκληρής δουλειάς του πατέρα του
items.snippet: Μια φιάλη υγραερίου έγινε σύμβολο αγάπης και αναγνώρισης σε μια τελετή αποφοίτησης.

items.snippet: Η Σέλτικ φέρεται να ψάχνεται για τη μεταγραφή του Πενράις.

items.title: NBA: Ο Χίρο «τρελάθηκε» που θα παίξει στους Μπακς
items.snippet: Ο Τάιλερ Χίρο δεν μπόρεσε να κρύψει τη χαρά του που θα φορέσει τη φανέλα των Μπακς.

items.snippet: Ο κόουτς Μεντιλίμπαρ διέκοψε πάνω από 2 φορές δίτερμα για να κάνει παρατηρήσεις ενώ ο Σιπιόνι συνεχίζει εκτός. ΟΛΛΑΝΔΙΑ - ΑΠΟΣΤΟΛΗ: ΔΗΜΗΤΡΗΣ ΤΟΜΑΡΑΣ.

items.title: Παράνομο streaming-μαμούθ με 120 ιστοσελίδες και 1,6 δισ. θεατές: Στη φυλακή οι δημιουργοί του παράνομου δικτύου
items.snippet: Παράνομες μεταδόσεις αγώνων, κρυπτονομίσματα και εκατομμύρια έσοδα στο επίκεντρο της υπόθεσης.

items.snippet: Ο Ελ Καμπί μπορεί να έγινε ο απόλυτος πόλος έλξης και στις 2 προπονήσεις της Τρίτης αλλά και ο Μεντιλίμπαρ είχε την τιμητική του. ΟΛΛΑΝΔΙΑ - ΑΠΟΣΤΟΛΗ: ΔΗΜΗΤΡΗΣ ΤΟΜΑΡΑΣ.

items.snippet: Ο Παναθηναϊκός θα έχει σχεδόν όλο το ΟΑΚΑ διαθέσιμο στη ρεβάνς με την Πάκσι.

items.snippet: Σύμφωνα με τον Ντέιβιντ Όρνσταϊν, η Αλ Χιλάλ συμφώνησε με τη Γουεστ Χαμ για την απόκτηση του Κρισένσιο Σάμερβιλ, προσφέροντας «γη και ύδωρ» στον αγγλικό σύλλογο.

items.title: Champions League: Επεισόδια στο Φενέρμπαχτσε - Γκόρνικ Ζάμπρζε

lead.title: Cristo Fernandez Talks Returning to Pro Soccer After “Ted Lasso”
lead.snippet: Cristo Fernandez was a soccer player before he was an actor. After finding TV stardom, he’s making the unlikely return to his first career.

items.title: Monkeys Understand Shapes in Almost the Same Way as Humans
items.snippet: Our geometric intuition, long considered something that sets us apart, seems to be shared with other primates.

items.title: 7 Other ‘Odyssey’ Movie Adaptations to Stream Now
items.snippet: For decades, filmmakers have been borrowing plot lines and metaphors from Homer’s epic for adventures set in different eras and realities.

items.title: ‘Too Rare to Care’? A New Center for Rare Diseases Hopes to Change That
items.snippet: A new nonprofit wants to streamline gene therapy for diseases often avoided by pharmaceutical companies — making treatment more like a routine procedure than a bespoke drug.

items.title: Hot, Uncomfortable, Transcendent: Why We Can’t Quit Opera at Bayreuth
items.snippet: The Bayreuth Festival, where Wagner’s operas have been performed for 150 years, is inconvenient and unpleasant. Why does it have such a hold on us?

items.title: The Best Genetic Risk Tools Don’t Work Equally for Everyone
items.snippet: Genetic prediction models are poised to revolutionize medicine. But because they have been trained on European DNA, they threaten to widen health care disparities.

items.title: Like It or Not, Summer Means Storytime at Ballet Theater
items.snippet: The company wrapped up its Met season of safe war horses with multiple debuts and a slew of promotions, including the onstage appointment of three principals.

items.title: Trump Attacks National Academies Over Climate Chapter in Judges’ Manual
items.snippet: The president joined Republican officials who slammed the National Academies of Sciences for a chapter about climate science in an educational guide for judges.

items.title: ‘Les Misérables’ in Concert and More: A Show’s Never-Ending Life
items.snippet: Concert versions and touring productions keep the spirit of 19th-century French revolutionaries alive.

items.title: The Sewer Knows What You Ate
items.snippet: During the Covid-19 pandemic, wastewater testing became a virus warning system. Now, scientists are using the approach to understand how communities eat.

items.title: When Listening to Music Was a Team Sport
items.snippet: An exhibition at the Cooper Hewitt celebrates the glorious machines that gave us the soundtracks of our lives before streaming sites shoved them toward obsolescence.

items.title: As Natural Disasters Expand, a Disaster-Tracking App Is Booming
items.snippet: Known for tracking California wildfires, Watch Duty is moving beyond that mission. Millions of people are turning to it for help.

items.title: Seth Meyers Calls Trump ‘The Sports Reaper’
items.snippet: The late-night host joked that the president’s sports curse had struck again after he backed Lionel Messi of Argentina, which then lost the World Cup final to Spain.

items.title: A Mathematical Tribute to the Soccer Ball
items.snippet: As the World Cup final approaches, take a moment to appreciate the beautiful symmetries of humanity’s favorite truncated icosahedron.

items.title: Cinephiles Took Their Own Journeys to See ‘The Odyssey’ in IMAX 70mm
items.snippet: To see Christopher Nolan’s interpretation of an epic journey in his preferred IMAX 70mm format, admirers packed their bags and took out their passports.
